entities.description: kg refers to a knowledge graph  which is a data structure used for multi hop reasoning
llm is an example of a distinct entity name mentioned in the context of entity ambiguity
large language model is an example of a distinct entity name mentioned in the context of entity ambiguity
the gradient based entity resolution method is a novel approach proposed to address entity ambiguity by analyzing similarity distributions
multi hop reasoning is a task that relies on a high quality knowledge graph
entity ambiguity is a problem where distinct entities share similar names  compromising the knowledge graph
similarity distribution is the data pattern analyzed by the proposed method to identify sharp drops in scores
candidate entities are the potential matches analyzed to distinguish and merge coreferent entities
coreferent entities are distinct entities that refer to the same real world object and need to be merged
graph connectivity is a property of the knowledge graph that is ensured by the proposed method
reasoning capabilities are the skills of the system that are enhanced by the proposed method

relations.description: the gradient based entity resolution method is proposed to ensure the high quality of the kg by resolving entity ambiguity
llm and large language model are cited as examples of distinct entities that cause ambiguity in the kg
multi hop reasoning relies on a high quality kg for its execution
entity ambiguity compromises the quality of the kg
the method analyzes the similarity distribution of candidate entities to function
the method analyzes candidate entities to identify sharp drops in similarity scores
the method distinguishes and merges coreferent entities
the method ensures graph connectivity by resolving entity ambiguity
the method enhances reasoning capabilities by improving the kg
llm is an example of a name that contributes to entity ambiguity
large language model is an example of a name that contributes to entity ambiguity
